vulnerabilities.title: Quill is vulnerable to XSS via HTML export feature
vulnerabilities.description:
A lack of data validation vulnerability in the HTML export feature in Quill in allows Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).

This issue affects Quill: 2.0.3.

vulnerabilities.title: Cross-site Scripting in quill
vulnerabilities.description:
A vulnerability in the HTML editor of Slab Quill allows an attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript by storing an XSS payload (a crafted `onloadstart` attribute of an IMG element) in a text field. No patch exists and no further releases are planned.

This CVE is disputed. Researchers have claimed that this issue is not within the product itself, but is intended behavior in a web browser. More information can be found [here](https://github.com/quilljs/quill/issues/3364).

vulnerabilities.title: Reverse Tabnabbing in quill
vulnerabilities.description:
Versions of `quill` prior to 1.3.7 are vulnerable to [Reverse Tabnabbing](https://www.owasp.org/index.php/Reverse_Tabnabbing). The package uses `target='_blank'` in anchor tags, allowing attackers to access `window.opener` for the original page when opening links. This is commonly used for phishing attacks.


## Recommendation

No fix is currently available. Consider using an alternative package until a fix is made available.
